title: cephalotaxus harringtonia var. drupacea
intro: Cephalotaxus harringtonia var. drupacea, also known as the Chinese plum yew, is a plant species within the Taxaceae family, characterized by its evergreen foliage and red arils that contain edible seeds. The plant is traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antitumor effects. The health benefits of Cephalotaxus harringtonia var. drupacea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are said to provide a range of health benefits and are used to treat various ailments.
